You have noticed that no changes have been made to the existing regression test cases for several iterations and no new regression defects were identified. Your manager is happy, but you are not. Which testing principle explains your skepticism?
opts: Tests wear out
Absence-of-defects fallacy
Defects cluster together
Exhaustive testing is impossible
exp: a) Is correct. This principle means that if the same tests are repeated over and over again, eventually these tests no longer find any new defects. This is probably why the tests all passed in this release as well. b) Is not correct. This principle says about the mistaken belief that just finding and fixing a large number of defects will ensure the success of a system. c) Is not correct. This principle says that a small number of components usually contain most of the defects. d) Is not correct. This principle states that testing all combinations of inputs and preconditions is not feasible.

q: You work in a team that develops a mobile application for food ordering. In the current iteration the team decided to implement the payment functionality. Which of the following activities is a part of test analysis?
opts: Estimating that testing the integration with the payment service will take 8 person-days
Deciding that the team should test if it is possible to properly share payment between many users
Using boundary value analysis (BVA) to derive the test data for the test cases that check the correct payment processing for the minimum allowed amount to be paid
Analyzing the discrepancy between the actual result and expected result after executing a test case that checks the process of payment with a credit card, and reporting a defect
exp: a) Is not correct. Estimating the test effort is part of test planning. b) Is correct. This is an example of defining test conditions which is a part of test analysis. c) Is not correct. Using test techniques to derive coverage items is a part of test design. d) Is not correct. Reporting defects found during dynamic testing is a part of test execution.

q: Which of the following factors have a SIGNIFICANT influence on the test approach? i. The SDLC  ii. The number of defects detected in previous projects  iii. The identified product risks  iv. New regulatory requirements forcing formal white-box testing  v. The test environment setup
opts: i, ii have significant influence
i, iii, iv have significant influence
ii, iv, v have significant influence
iii, v have significant influence
exp: i. Is true. The SDLC has an influence on the test approach. ii. Is false. The number of defects detected in previous projects may have some influence, but this is not as significant. iii. Is true. The identified product risks are one of the most important factors. iv. Is true. Regulatory requirements are important factors. v. Is false. The test environment has no significant influence on the test approach.

q: Which TWO of the following tasks belong MAINLY to a testing role?
opts: Configure test environments
Maintain the product backlog
Design solutions to new requirements
Create the test plan
Analyze the test basis
exp: a) Is correct. This is done by the testers. b) Is not correct. The product backlog is built and maintained by the product owner. c) Is not correct. This is done by the development team. d) Is not correct. This is a managerial role. e) Is correct. This is done by the testers since it is a technical task done as part of test analysis.

q: Which of the following skills (i-v) are the MOST important skills of a tester? i. Having domain knowledge  ii. Creating a product vision  iii. Being a good team player  iv. Planning and organizing the work of the team  v. Critical thinking
opts: ii and iv are important
i, iii and v are important
i, ii and v are important
iii and iv are important
exp: i. Is true. Having domain knowledge is an important tester skill. ii. Is false. This is a task of the business analyst together with the business representative. iii. Is true. Being a good team player is an important skill. iv. Is false. Planning and organizing the work of the team is a task of the test manager, not just the tester. v. Is true. Critical thinking is one of the most important skills of testers.

q: How is the whole team approach present in the interactions between testers and business representatives?
opts: Business representatives decide on test automation approaches
Testers help business representatives to define a test strategy
Business representatives are not part of the whole team approach
Testers help business representatives to create suitable acceptance tests
exp: a) Is not correct. The test automation approach is defined by testers with the help of developers and business representatives. b) Is not correct. The test strategy is decided in collaboration with the developers. c) Is not correct. Testers, developers, and business representatives are all part of the whole team approach. d) Is correct. Testers will work closely with business representatives to ensure desired quality levels are achieved, including supporting and collaborating with them to help create suitable acceptance tests.

q: Consider the following rule: 'for every SDLC activity there is a corresponding test activity'. In which SDLC models does this rule hold?
opts: Only in sequential development models
Only in iterative development models
Only in iterative and incremental development models
In sequential, incremental, and iterative development models
exp: d) Is correct. This rule holds for all SDLC models.

q: Which of the following statements BEST describes the acceptance test-driven development (ATDD) approach?
opts: In ATDD, acceptance criteria are typically created based on the given/when/then format
In ATDD, test cases are mainly created at component testing and are code-oriented
In ATDD, tests are created, based on acceptance criteria to drive the development of the related software
In ATDD, tests are based on the desired behavior of the software, which makes it easier for team members to understand them
exp: a) Is not correct. The given/when/then format is more often used in behavior-driven development (BDD). b) Is not correct. This is the description of test-driven development (TDD). c) Is correct. In ATDD, tests are written from acceptance criteria as part of the design process. d) Is not correct. This is used in BDD.

q: Which of the following is NOT an example of the shift-left approach?
opts: Reviewing the user requirements before they are formally accepted by the stakeholders
Writing a component test before the corresponding code is written
Executing a performance efficiency test for a component during component testing
Writing a test script before setting up the configuration management process
exp: a) Is not correct. Early review is an example of the shift-left approach. b) Is not correct. TDD is an example of the shift-left approach. c) Is not correct. Early non-functional testing is an example of the shift-left approach. d) Is correct. Test scripts should be subject to configuration management, so it makes no sense to create them before this process is set up.

q: Which of the arguments below would you use to convince your manager to organize retrospectives at the end of each release cycle?
opts: Retrospectives are very popular these days and clients would appreciate it if we added them to our processes
Organizing retrospectives will save the organization money because without them end user representatives do not provide immediate feedback about the product
Process weaknesses identified during the retrospective can be analyzed and serve as a to do list for the organization's continuous process improvement program
Retrospectives embrace five values including courage and respect, which are crucial to maintain continuous improvement in the organization
exp: a) Is not correct. Retrospectives are more useful for identifying improvement opportunities and have little importance for clients. b) Is not correct. Retrospectives are not aimed to collect feedback about the product, but about the process. c) Is correct. Regularly conducted retrospectives, when appropriate follow-up activities occur, are critical to continual improvement of development and testing. d) Is not correct. Courage and respect are values of Extreme Programming and are not closely related to retrospectives.

q: Which types of failures (1-4) fit which test levels (A-D) BEST? 1. Failures in system behavior as it deviates from the user's business needs  2. Failures in communication between components  3. Failures in logic in the code  4. Failures in not correctly implemented business rules  /  A. Component testing  B. Component integration testing  C. System testing  D. Acceptance testing
exp: The test basis for acceptance testing is the user's business needs (1-D). Communication between components is tested during component integration testing (2-B). Failures in logic can be found during component testing (3-A). Business rules are the test basis for system testing (4-C).

q: You are testing a user story with three acceptance criteria: AC1 (TC1), AC2 (TC2), AC3 (TC3). Execution 1: TC1 Failed, TC2 Passed, TC3 Failed. Execution 2: TC1 Passed, TC2 Failed, TC3 Failed. Execution 3: TC1 Passed, TC2 Passed, TC3 Passed. Tests are repeated once all defects found are corrected. Which of the above tests are executed as regression tests?
opts: Only 5, 7
Only 5, 6
exp: Because TC1 and TC3 failed in Execution 1 (tests 1 and 3), tests 4 and 6 are confirmation tests. Because TC2 and TC3 failed in Execution 2 (tests 5 and 6), tests 8 and 9 are also confirmation tests. TC2 passed in Execution 1 (test 2), so test 5 is a regression test. TC1 passed in Execution 2 (test 4), so test 7 is also a regression test.

q: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of static testing?
opts: Having less expensive defect management due to the ease of detecting defects later in the SDLC
Fixing defects found during static testing is generally much less expensive than fixing defects found during dynamic testing
Finding coding defects that might not have been found by only performing dynamic testing
Detecting gaps and inconsistencies in requirements
exp: a) Is correct (NOT a benefit). Defect management is no less expensive. Finding and fixing defects later in the SDLC is actually more costly. b), c), d) Are all genuine benefits of static testing.

q: Which of the following is a benefit of early and frequent feedback?
opts: It improves the test process for future projects
It forces customers to prioritize their requirements based on agreed risks
It provides a measure for the quality of changes
It helps avoid requirements misunderstandings
exp: a) Is not correct. Feedback can improve the test process, but if one only wants to improve future projects, the feedback does not need to come early or frequently. b) Is not correct. Feedback is not used to prioritize requirements. c) Is not correct. There is no one recommended way to measure quality of changes. d) Is correct. Early and frequent feedback can prevent misunderstandings about requirements.

q: The reviews being used in your organization have the following attributes: there is the role of a scribe, the main purpose is to evaluate quality, the meeting is led by the author of the work product, there is individual preparation, a review report is produced. Which of the following review types is MOST likely being used?
opts: Informal review
Walkthrough
Technical review
Inspection
exp: A scribe is specified for walkthroughs, technical reviews, and inspections — so this cannot be an informal review. The purpose of evaluating quality is one of the most important objectives of a walkthrough. The meeting being led by the author is not allowed for inspections and is typically not done in technical reviews, but is characteristic of walkthroughs.

q: Which of these statements is NOT a factor that contributes to successful reviews?
opts: Participants should dedicate adequate time for the review
Splitting large work products into small parts to make the required effort less intense
Participants should avoid behaviors that might indicate boredom, exasperation, or hostility to other participants
Failures found should be acknowledged, appreciated, and handled objectively
exp: a), b), c) Are all genuine success factors for reviews. d) Is correct (NOT a success factor). During reviews one can find defects, not failures. The statement incorrectly uses the term 'failures' instead of 'defects'.

q: Which of the following is a characteristic of experience-based test techniques?
opts: Test cases are created based on detailed design information
Items tested within the interface code section are used to measure coverage
The test techniques heavily rely on the tester's knowledge of the software and the business domain
The test cases are used to identify deviations from the requirements
exp: a) Is not correct. This is a characteristic of white-box test techniques. b) Is not correct. This is a characteristic of white-box test techniques (coverage measurement). c) Is correct. This knowledge and experience includes expected use of the software, its environment, likely defects, and their distribution. d) Is not correct. This is a characteristic of black-box test techniques.

q: You are testing a simplified apartment search form with floor (ground floor; first floor; second or higher floor) and garden type (no garden; small garden; large garden). Ground floor apartments have a garden, higher floors don't. What is the minimal number of test cases to achieve 100% EP coverage for valid partitions?
exp: 'Small garden' and 'large garden' can go only with 'ground floor', so we need two test cases with 'ground floor' covering these two garden type partitions. We need two more test cases to cover the two other floor partitions. The 'no garden' partition is covered by these tests. Total: TC1 (ground floor, small garden), TC2 (ground floor, large garden), TC3 (first floor, no garden), TC4 (second or higher floor, no garden).

q: You are testing a system that calculates final course grade: 0-50 failed, 51-60 fair, 61-70 satisfactory, 71-80 good, 81-90 very good, 91-100 excellent. Test cases: TC1=91, TC2=50, TC3=81, TC4=60, TC5=70, TC6=80. What is the 2-value BVA coverage for the final result?
exp: There are 12 boundary values: 0, 50, 51, 60, 61, 70, 71, 80, 81, 90, 91, and 100. The test cases cover six of them: TC1=91, TC2=50, TC3=81, TC4=60, TC5=70, TC6=80. Therefore coverage = 6/12 = 50%.

q: A bicycle rental CRM: members get 20% discount (unless deadline missed); after 15 rentals members get a T-Shirt gift. Decision table R1-R8 (Being a member T/F, Missed deadline T/F, 15th rental T/F). Based ONLY on the feature description, which rule describes an impossible situation?
opts: R4
R2
R6
R8
exp: R8 = non-member, missed deadline, 15th rental. A non-member gets no discount (correct) but the T-Shirt gift is only for members. Therefore R8 shows an impossible action: a non-member cannot receive a T-Shirt gift regardless of the number of rentals.

q: You test a system whose lifecycle is modeled by a state transition diagram. The system starts in the INIT state and ends in the OFF state. What is the MINIMAL number of test cases to achieve valid transitions coverage?
exp: 'test' and 'error' transitions cannot occur in one test case. Neither can both 'done' transitions. This means we need at least three test cases. For example: TC1: test, done. TC2: run, error, done. TC3: run, pause, resume, pause, done.

q: Your test suite achieved 100% statement coverage. What is the consequence of this fact?
opts: Each instruction in the code that contains a defect has been executed at least once
Any test suite containing more test cases than your test suite will also achieve 100% statement coverage
Each path in the code has been executed at least once
Every combination of input values has been tested at least once
exp: a) Is correct. Since 100% statement coverage is achieved, every statement, including those with defects, must have been executed at least once. b) Is not correct. Coverage depends on what is tested, not on the number of test cases. c) Is not correct. If there is a loop in the code there may be an infinite number of paths. d) Is not correct. Exhaustive testing is not possible.

q: Which of the following is NOT true for white-box testing?
opts: During white-box testing the entire software implementation is considered
White-box coverage metrics can help identify additional tests to increase code coverage
White-box test techniques can be used in static testing
White-box testing can help identify gaps in requirements implementation
exp: a), b), c) Are all true for white-box testing. d) Is correct (NOT true). This is a weakness of white-box test techniques — they are not able to identify missing implementation because they are based solely on the test object structure, not on the requirements specification.

q: Which of the following BEST describes the concept behind error guessing?
opts: Error guessing involves using your knowledge and experience of defects found in the past and typical errors made by developers
Error guessing involves using your personal experience of development and the errors you made as a developer
Error guessing requires you to imagine that you are the user of the test object and to guess errors the user could make interacting with it
Error guessing requires you to rapidly duplicate the development task to identify the sort of errors a developer might make
exp: a) Is correct. The basic concept behind error guessing is that the tester tries to guess what errors may have been made by the developer and what defects may be in the test object based on past experience. b) Is not correct. Error guessing is not based solely on prior development experience. c) Is not correct. Error guessing is not a usability technique. d) Is not correct. Duplicating the development task is impractical and is not error guessing.

q: In your project there has been a delay in the release of a brand-new application and test execution started late, but you have very detailed domain knowledge and good analytical skills. The full list of requirements has not yet been shared with the team, but management is asking for some test results. Which test technique fits BEST?
opts: Checklist-based testing
Error guessing
Exploratory testing
Branch testing
exp: a) Is not correct. This is a new product; you probably do not have a checklist yet and test conditions might not be known. b) Is not correct. This is a new product; you probably do not have enough information to make correct error guesses. c) Is correct. Exploratory testing is most useful when there are few known specifications and/or there is a pressing timeline. d) Is not correct. Branch testing is time-consuming and does not leverage domain knowledge effectively.

q: Which of the following BEST describes the way acceptance criteria can be documented?
opts: Performing retrospectives to determine the actual needs of the stakeholders regarding a given user story
Using the given/when/then format to describe an example test condition related to a given user story
Using verbal communication to reduce the risk of misunderstanding the acceptance criteria by others
Documenting risks related to a given user story in a test plan to facilitate the risk-based testing of a given user story
exp: a) Is not correct. Retrospectives are for process improvement, not for documenting acceptance criteria. b) Is correct. This is the standard way to document acceptance criteria. c) Is not correct. Verbal communication does not allow physically documenting acceptance criteria. d) Is not correct. Acceptance criteria are the conditions for completion, not risks.

q: User story: As an Editor I want to review content before it is published. Acceptance criteria: log in with Editor role, view/edit pages, add markup comments, save changes, reassign to content owner. Which is the BEST example of an ATDD test for this user story?
opts: Test if the editor can save the document after editing the page content
Test if the content owner can log in and make updates to the content
Test if the editor can schedule the edited content for publication
Test if the editor can reassign to another editor to make updates
exp: a) Is correct. This test covers two acceptance criteria: editing the document and saving changes. b) Is not correct. Acceptance criteria cover the editor activities, not the content owner. c) Is not correct. Scheduling for publication is not covered by the acceptance criteria. d) Is not correct. Acceptance criteria state reassigning from editor to content owner, not to another editor.

q: How do testers add value to iteration and release planning?
opts: Testers determine the priority of the user stories to be developed
Testers focus only on the functional aspects of the system to be tested
Testers participate in the detailed risk identification and risk assessment of user stories
Testers guarantee the release of high-quality software through early test design during the release planning
exp: a) Is not correct. Priorities for user stories are determined by the business representative together with the development team. b) Is not correct. Testers focus on both functional and non-functional aspects. c) Is correct. According to the syllabus, this is one of the ways testers add value to iteration and release planning. d) Is not correct. Early test design is not part of release planning and does not automatically guarantee quality.

q: Which TWO of the following options are the exit criteria for testing a system?
opts: Test environment readiness
The ability to log in to the test object by the tester
Estimated defect density is reached
Requirements are translated into given/when/then format
Regression tests are automated
exp: a) Is not correct. Test environment readiness is a resource availability criterion (entry criteria). b) Is not correct. This is also an entry criterion. c) Is correct. Estimated defect density is a measure of diligence — exit criteria. d) Is not correct. Testable requirements belong to entry criteria. e) Is correct. Automation of regression tests is a completion criterion — exit criteria.

q: Your team uses the three-point estimation technique. Most optimistic: 2 person-hours, most likely: 11 person-hours, most pessimistic: 14 person-hours. What is the final estimate?
opts: 9 person-hours
14 person-hours
11 person-hours
10 person-hours
exp: Three-point estimation formula: E = (optimistic + 4*most likely + pessimistic) / 6 = (2 + 4*11 + 14) / 6 = (2 + 44 + 14) / 6 = 60 / 6 = 10 person-hours.

q: Test cases: TC001 (Select food, priority 3, no dep), TC002 (Select restaurant, priority 2, dep TC001), TC003 (Get direction, priority 1, dep TC002), TC004 (Call restaurant, priority 2, dep TC002), TC005 (Make reservation, priority 3, dep TC002). Which test case should be executed as the THIRD one?
opts: TC 003
TC 005
TC 002
TC 001
exp: TC001 must come first (no dependencies). TC002 comes second (depends on TC001). After TC002, TC003 has the highest priority (priority 1) among remaining tests, so it runs third. Order: TC001 → TC002 → TC003 → TC004 → TC005.

q: Match test categories to agile testing quadrants: 1. Usability testing  2. Component testing  3. Functional testing  4. Reliability testing  /  A=Q1 technology-facing supporting dev  B=Q2 business-facing supporting dev  C=Q3 business-facing critique product  D=Q4 technology-facing critique product
exp: Usability testing is business-facing and critiques the product → Q3 (C). Component testing is technology-facing and supports development → Q1 (A). Functional testing is business-facing and supports development → Q2 (B). Reliability testing is technology-facing and critiques the product → Q4 (D).

q: Risk: Response time too long for reports. Likelihood: medium, Impact: high. Response: independent team performs performance testing during system testing + end users perform alpha/beta testing before release. What measure is proposed?
opts: Risk acceptance
Contingency plan
Risk mitigation
Risk transfer
exp: a) Is not correct. We do not accept the risk; concrete actions are proposed. b) Is not correct. No contingency plans are proposed. c) Is correct. The proposed actions are related to testing, which is a form of risk mitigation — actively working to reduce the likelihood or impact of the risk. d) Is not correct. Risk is not transferred to another party but mitigated through testing.

q: Which work product can be used by an agile team to show the amount of work that has been completed and the amount of total work remaining for a given iteration?
opts: Acceptance criteria
Defect report
Test completion report
Burndown chart
exp: a) Is not correct. Acceptance criteria are conditions to decide if a user story is ready; they cannot show work progress. b) Is not correct. Defect reports inform about defects, not work progress. c) Is not correct. Test completion report is created after the iteration is finished, so it does not show continuous progress within an iteration. d) Is correct. Burndown charts are a graphical representation of work left to do versus time remaining, updated daily.

q: You need to update one of the automated test scripts to be in line with a new requirement. Which process indicates that you create a new version of the test script in the test repository?
opts: Traceability management
Maintenance testing
Configuration management
Requirements engineering
exp: a) Is not correct. Traceability is the relationship between two or more work products, not between different versions of the same work product. b) Is not correct. Maintenance testing is about testing changes, not related closely to versioning. c) Is correct. Configuration management may involve the version control of all test items, including test scripts. d) Is not correct. Requirements engineering is about elicitation, documentation, and management of requirements.

q: A defect report states: 'Application hangs up after entering Test input: $ä in the Name field. Tried with test_admin01 and other admin accounts. No error message; log contains fatal error. Based on TC-1305, application should accept input and create the user. Please fix with high priority, related to REQ-0012.' Developers say it is not reproducible. What critical information is MISSING?
opts: Expected result and actual result
References and defect status
Test environment and test item
Priority and severity
exp: a) Is not correct. Expected result ('should accept input and create user') and actual result ('application hangs up') are both present. b) Is not correct. References (TC-1305, REQ-0012) and defect status (Rejected) are present. c) Is correct. We do not know in which test environment the anomaly was detected, nor which application version is affected — this is critical for reproduction. d) Is not correct. Priority (high) is stated, and severity can be inferred from the impact described.

q: Which test activity does a data preparation tool support?
opts: Test monitoring and test control
Test analysis
Test design and test implementation
Test completion
exp: a) Is not correct. Test monitoring involves checking activities and comparing actual progress against the plan — no test data preparation occurs. b) Is not correct. Test analysis includes identifying and prioritizing test conditions — no data preparation. c) Is correct. Test design and implementation can include the identification, creation, or acquisition of testware necessary for test execution, such as test data. d) Is not correct. Test completion activities occur at project milestones — too late for preparing test data.

q: Which item correctly identifies a potential risk of performing test automation?
opts: It may introduce unknown regressions in production
Sufficient efforts to maintain testware may not be properly allocated
Testing tools and associated testware may not be sufficiently relied upon
It may reduce the time allocated for manual testing
exp: a) Is not correct. Test automation does not introduce unknown regressions in production. b) Is correct. Wrong allocation of effort to maintain testware is a genuine risk of test automation. c) Is not correct. Test tools must be selected so that they can be relied upon. d) Is not correct. Reducing manual testing is actually a benefit of test automation, not a risk.

q: You were given a task to analyze and fix causes of failures in a new system to be released. Which activity are you performing?
opts: Debugging
Software testing
Requirement elicitation
Defect management
exp: a) Is correct. Debugging is the process of finding, analyzing, and removing the causes of failures in a component or system. b) Is not correct. Testing is about planning, preparation and evaluation — not about fixing causes. c) Is not correct. Requirement elicitation is gathering and capturing requirements. d) Is not correct. Defect management is recognizing, recording, classifying, investigating, resolving, and disposing of defects — not fixing the root cause.

q: In many software organizations the test department is called the Quality Assurance (QA) department. Is this sentence correct or not and why?
opts: It is correct. Testing and QA mean exactly the same thing
It is correct. These names can be used interchangeably because both testing and QA focus on the same quality issues
It is not correct. Testing is something more; testing includes all activities with regard to quality. QA focuses on quality-related processes
It is not correct. QA is focused on quality-related processes while testing concentrates on demonstrating that a component or system is fit for purpose and to detect defects
exp: Testing and quality assurance are not the same. Testing is the process consisting of all SDLC activities, both static and dynamic, concerned with planning, preparation and evaluation of a component or system. Quality assurance is focused on establishing, introducing, monitoring, improving, and adhering to quality-related processes.

q: A phone ringing distracts a programmer causing him to improperly program the logic that checks the upper boundary of an input variable. Later, a tester notices that the input field accepts invalid values. Which of the following correctly describes an incorrectly coded upper bound?
opts: The root cause
A failure
An error
A defect
exp: a) Is not correct. The root cause is the distraction the programmer experienced. b) Is not correct. Accepting invalid inputs is the failure (observable incorrect behavior). c) Is not correct. The error is the mistake in thinking that put the defect in the code. d) Is correct. The incorrectly coded upper bound in the code is the defect (fault/bug).

q: Consider the following testware — Test Charter #04.018, Session time: 1h, Explore: Registration page, With: Different sets of incorrect input data, To discover: Defects related to accepting the registration process with incorrect input. Which test activity produces this testware as an output?
opts: Test planning
Test monitoring and test control
Test analysis
Test design
exp: The testware under consideration is a test charter. Test charters are the output from test design.

q: Which of the following is the BEST example of how traceability supports testing?
opts: Performing the impact analysis of a change will give information about the completion of the tests
Analyzing the traceability between test cases and test results will give information about the estimated level of residual risk
Performing the impact analysis of a change will help selecting the right test cases for regression testing
Analyzing the traceability between the test basis, the test objects and the test cases will help in selecting test data
exp: a) Is not correct. Impact analysis helps select test cases, not assess completion. b) Is not correct. Traceability to test results alone doesn't give residual risk information unless traced to risks. c) Is correct. Performing impact analysis of changes helps in selecting the right test cases for regression testing. d) Is not correct. Selecting test data is more related to test analysis and implementation, not traceability.

q: Which of the following BEST explains a benefit of independence of testing?
opts: The use of an independent test team allows project management to assign responsibility for quality to the test team
If a test team external to the organization can be afforded, there are distinct benefits in terms of not being swayed by delivery concerns
An independent test team can work separately from developers and restrict communication to defect reporting
When specifications contain ambiguities and inconsistencies, an independent tester can be useful in questioning assumptions and interpretations made by the developer
exp: a) Is not correct. Quality should be the responsibility of everyone, not solely the test team. b) Is not correct. External teams still need to meet delivery deadlines. c) Is not correct. Working in complete isolation and restricting communication is bad practice. d) Is correct. Specifications are never perfect; an independent tester can challenge and verify assumptions and interpretations made by the developer.

q: You are working as a tester in the team that follows the V-model. Which TWO of the following activities CAN be performed in the initial phases of the SDLC?
opts: Dynamic test execution
Static testing
Test planning
Acceptance test execution
Maintenance testing
exp: a) Is not correct. Dynamic test execution requires executable code, which is not available in initial phases. b) Is correct. In initial phases, testers participate in requirement reviews, which is a form of static testing. c) Is correct. Test planning could be performed early in the SDLC before testing begins. d) Is not correct. Acceptance test execution requires a working product. e) Is not correct. Maintenance testing requires a deployed product.

q: Which of the following are advantages of DevOps? i. Faster product release and faster time to market  ii. Increases the need for repetitive manual testing  iii. Constant availability of executable software  iv. Reduction in the number of regression tests  v. Setting up the test automation framework is inexpensive
opts: i, ii, iv are advantages
iii, v are advantages
i, iii are advantages
ii, iv, v are advantages
exp: i. Is true. Faster product release and faster time to market is a DevOps advantage. ii. Is false. DevOps typically reduces the need for repetitive manual testing through automation. iii. Is true. Constant availability of executable software is a DevOps advantage. iv. Is false. More regression tests are needed in DevOps, not fewer. v. Is false. Setting up a test automation framework is expensive.

q: A client requirement says: 'The order must be processed in less than 10 seconds in 95% of the cases'. You created test cases measuring order processing time. What test type did you perform?
opts: Functional, because the test cases cover the user's business requirement for the system
Non-functional, because they measure the system's performance
Functional, because the test cases interact with the user interface
White-box, because we need to know the internal structure of the program to measure the order processing time
exp: a) Is not correct. The fact that the requirement comes from the client doesn't make it functional; these tests check 'how fast' (performance), not 'what' the system does. b) Is correct. This is an example of testing for performance efficiency, a type of non-functional testing. c) Is not correct. Even if the UI is used, the main objective is checking performance. d) Is not correct. We don't need internal structure knowledge to perform performance efficiency testing.

q: Your organization's test strategy suggests that once a system is going to be retired, data migration shall be tested. As part of what test type is this testing MOST likely to be performed?
opts: Maintenance testing
Regression testing
Reliability testing
Integration testing
exp: a) Is correct. When a system is retired, this can require testing of data migration, which is a form of maintenance testing. b) Is not correct. Regression testing verifies whether a fix accidentally affected other parts of the code. c) Is not correct. Portability testing focuses on transferring the system to another environment. d) Is not correct. Integration testing focuses on interactions between components, not data migration.

q: Which of the following work products can be reviewed? i. Business requirements  ii. Schedule  iii. Test budget  iv. Third-party executable code  v. User stories and their acceptance criteria
opts: i and iv can be reviewed
i, ii, iii and iv can be reviewed
i, ii, iii, and v can be reviewed
iii, iv, v can be reviewed
exp: Only third-party executable code (iv) cannot be reviewed through static testing, since the source code is not available. All other work products — business requirements, schedule, test budget, and user stories — can be reviewed.

q: Which of the following statements (i-v) are true for static testing? i. Abnormal external behaviors are easier to identify  ii. Discrepancies from a coding standard are easier to find  iii. It identifies failures caused by defects when the software is run  iv. Its test objective is to identify defects as early as possible  v. Missing coverage for critical security requirements is easier to find and fix
opts: i, iv, v are true for static testing
i, iii, iv are true for static testing
ii, iii are true for static testing
ii, iv, v are true for static testing
exp: i. Is false. Abnormal external behaviors are detectable while the software is running — that's dynamic testing. ii. Is true. Deviations from coding standards are a typical defect found with static testing. iii. Is false. Identifying failures when software runs is dynamic testing, not static. iv. Is true. Identifying defects as early as possible is a test objective of static testing. v. Is true. Gaps in security requirements traceability are a typical defect found with static testing.
